caption: Step 1 — the original screenshot, as the parser first sees it.
Step 2 — the puzzle-locator. A warm-beige board container is detected (purple rectangle); the horizontal separator rule (line) divides board from domino tray.

Step 3 — the cell lattice. Cell pitch is recovered from the autocorrelation of inter-cell brightness valleys, then the grid phase is locked by sliding it to maximise tile coverage. Each solid square is a detected cell, with a dot at its centre.
Step 4 — region segmentation. Orthogonally-adjacent cells of near-equal colour are merged into one region; the letter marks distinct regions.

Step 5 — constraint tags. Saturated diamonds carry a white-on-colour glyph; that glyph is isolated, split into characters and matched against a small template library. Each tag is then Hungarian-assigned to its region.
Step 6 — the tray. Each domino tile is detected as a near-white rounded rectangle; the tile is split in two and the dark pip blobs counted on each half.
Step 7 — energy-model solver: 0 of 4 dominoes placed. The solver walks tilings depth-first, pruning the instant any region's per-region energy lower bound exceeds zero.
Step 7 — energy-model solver: 1 of 4 dominoes placed. The solver walks tilings depth-first, pruning the instant any region's per-region energy lower bound exceeds zero.
Step 7 — energy-model solver: 2 of 4 dominoes placed. The solver walks tilings depth-first, pruning the instant any region's per-region energy lower bound exceeds zero.
Step 7 — energy-model solver: 3 of 4 dominoes placed. The solver walks tilings depth-first, pruning the instant any region's per-region energy lower bound exceeds zero.
Step 7 — energy-model solver: 4 of 4 dominoes placed. The solver walks tilings depth-first, pruning the instant any region's per-region energy lower bound exceeds zero.
